conflictGroups.world_shape: A group of scenarios cosntrianing the shape of the world graph. Combinations are contradictory by nature, and will always be UNSAT, and are disallowed.

scenarios.name: Linear Branch
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a linear story.
scenarios.alloyTemplate: (one w: World | no next.w) and (one w: World | no w.next) and (all w: World | lone w.next)
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a linear story.

scenarios.name: Alternative Futures
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see different outcomes for a same situation.
scenarios.alloyTemplate: one w: World | no next.w and #w.next >= 2 and (all w2: World | w != w2 implies w2 in w.next)
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see different outcomes for a same situation.

scenarios.name: Counterfactual Worlds
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see that things may have taken a different outcome in the past.
scenarios.alloyTemplate: some w1, w2: World | w1 != w2 and next.w1 = next.w2 and (some w1.next or some w2.next)
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see that things may have taken a different outcome in the past.

scenarios.name: Branch Depth
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story composed of [at least / at most / exactly] [n] consecutive worlds.
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story composed of <<depth_quantifier>> <<world_count>> consecutive worlds.

scenarios.name: Population Size
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story with [at least / at most / exactly] [n] individuals.
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story with <<count_comparison>> <<n>> individuals.

scenarios.name: Population Variability
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story where every moment [has the same / has different] [objects / properties / individuals].
scenarios.alloyTemplate: all w1, w2: World | w2 != w1 implies w1.exists<<aspect>> <<comparison>> w2.exists<<aspect>>
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story where every moment <<comparison>> <<aspect>>.

scenarios.name: Population Growth
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story exclusively composed by individuals [coming to existence / ceasing to exist].
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story exclusively composed by individuals <<direction>>.

choices.name: coming into existence
choices.alloySnippet: all w1, w2: World | w2 in w1.next implies w1.exists in w2.exists

choices.name: ceasing to exist
choices.alloySnippet: all w1, w2: World | w2 in w1.next implies w2.exists in w1.exists

scenarios.name: Extension Size
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story composed [only / at least / at most] by worlds with [at least / at most / exactly] [n] instances of [Class].
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story <<world_quantifier>> with <<count_comparison>> <<n>> instances of <<class_name>>.

scenarios.name: Temporal Extension Size
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story with [at least / at most / exactly] [n] instances of [Class].
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story with <<count_comparison>> <<n>> instances of <<class_name>> across all worlds.

parameters.name: Number of instances across all worlds

scenarios.name: Extension Variability
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story where the set of instances of [Class] [always / never] changes from world to world.
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story where the set of instances of <<class_name>> <<variability>>.

choices.name: always changes
choices.naturalLanguageSnippet: always changes from world to world

choices.name: never changes
choices.naturalLanguageSnippet: never changes from world to world

scenarios.name: Extension Comparison
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story where, in [all / some / at least / at most / exactly] [n] world(s), the extension of [Class1] is [(not) equal to / (not) included in / (not) disjoint from] the extension of [Class2].
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story where, <<world_quantifier>>, the extension of <<class1>> is <<set_relation>> the extension of <<class2>>.

scenarios.name: Extension Size Comparison
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story where, in [all / some / at least / at most / exactly] [n] world(s), the number of [Class1] is [equal / greater / lesser] than the number of [Class2].
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story where, <<world_quantifier>>, the number of <<class1>> is <<size_comparison>> the number of <<class2>>.

scenarios.name: Multiple Instantiation
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story where, in [at least / at most / exactly] [n] worlds, there are [at least / at most / exactly] [m] individuals that simultaneously instantiate [Class1, Class2]+.
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story where, in <<world_count_comparison>> <<world_count>> worlds, there are <<individual_count_comparison>> <<m>> individuals that simultaneously instantiate <<class1>> and <<class2>>.

scenarios.name: Temporal Multiple Instantiation
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story where [n] individuals instantiate [Class1, Class2] throughout time.
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story where <<n>> individuals instantiate <<class1>> and <<class2>> throughout time.

scenarios.name: Exclusive Instantiation
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story where, in [all / some / at least / at most / exactly] [n] world(s), an instance of [CommonType] must instantiate only one of the following classes: [AntiRigidClass].
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story where, <<world_quantifier>>, any instance of <<common_type>> instantiates only one of <<class1>> or <<class2>>.

choices.name: in every world
choices.alloySnippet: all w: World | all x: w.<<common_type>> | (x in w.<<class1>> or x in w.<<class2>>) and not (x in w.<<class1>> and x in w.<<class2>>)

choices.name: in some world
choices.alloySnippet: some w: World | all x: w.<<common_type>> | (x in w.<<class1>> or x in w.<<class2>>) and not (x in w.<<class1>> and x in w.<<class2>>)

choices.name: at least n worlds
choices.alloySnippet: #{w: World | all x: w.<<common_type>> | (x in w.<<class1>> or x in w.<<class2>>) and not (x in w.<<class1>> and x in w.<<class2>>)} >= <<world_count>>

choices.name: at most n worlds
choices.alloySnippet: #{w: World | all x: w.<<common_type>> | (x in w.<<class1>> or x in w.<<class2>>) and not (x in w.<<class1>> and x in w.<<class2>>)} =< <<world_count>>

choices.name: exactly n worlds
choices.alloySnippet: #{w: World | all x: w.<<common_type>> | (x in w.<<class1>> or x in w.<<class2>>) and not (x in w.<<class1>> and x in w.<<class2>>)} = <<world_count>>

scenarios.name: Mandatory Anti-Rigidity
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story where all objects cease to be an instance of [Candidate anti-rigid class].
scenarios.alloyTemplate: all x: World.<<class_name>> | some disj w1, w2: World | x in w1.exists and x in w1.<<class_name>> and x in w2.exists and x not in w2.<<class_name>>
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story where all objects cease to be an instance of <<class_name>>.

scenarios.name: Pseudo-Rigid
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story where individuals never cease to be an instance of [AntiRigidClass].
scenarios.alloyTemplate: all x: World.<<class_name>>, w: World | x in w.exists implies x in w.<<class_name>>
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story where individuals, while they exist, never cease to be an instance of <<class_name>>.

scenarios.name: Association Changeability
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story where, in [no / every / at least / at most / exactly] world(s), [no / every / at least / at most / exactly] instance(s) of [Class1 (source)] is/are connected to the [same / different] instances of [Class2 (target)], through association [Association].
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story where, <<world_quantifier>>, <<instance_quantifier>>.

choices.name: every
choices.naturalLanguageSnippet: every instance of <<class1>> is connected to <<target_comparison>> instances of <<class2>> through <<association>>

choices.name: no
choices.naturalLanguageSnippet: no instance of <<class1>> is connected to <<target_comparison>> instances of <<class2>> through <<association>>

choices.name: at least m
choices.naturalLanguageSnippet: at least <<m>> instance(s) of <<class1>> are connected to <<target_comparison>> instances of <<class2>> through <<association>>

choices.name: at most m
choices.naturalLanguageSnippet: at most <<m>> instance(s) of <<class1>> are connected to <<target_comparison>> instances of <<class2>> through <<association>>

choices.name: exactly m
choices.naturalLanguageSnippet: exactly <<m>> instance(s) of <<class1>> are connected to <<target_comparison>> instances of <<class2>> through <<association>>

choices.name: in every world
choices.alloySnippet: all w1, w2: World | (w1 != w2 and w1 in w2.^next and some w1.<<class1>> & w2.<<class1>>) implies (<<instance_quantifier>>)

choices.name: in no world
choices.alloySnippet: no w1, w2: World | w1 != w2 and w1 in w2.^next and some w1.<<class1>> & w2.<<class1>> and (<<instance_quantifier>>)

choices.name: at least n worlds
choices.alloySnippet: #{w1, w2: World | w1 != w2 and w1 in w2.^next and some w1.<<class1>> & w2.<<class1>> and (<<instance_quantifier>>)} >= <<world_count>>

choices.name: at most n worlds
choices.alloySnippet: #{w1, w2: World | w1 != w2 and w1 in w2.^next and some w1.<<class1>> & w2.<<class1>> and (<<instance_quantifier>>)} =< <<world_count>>

choices.name: exactly n worlds
choices.alloySnippet: #{w1, w2: World | w1 != w2 and w1 in w2.^next and some w1.<<class1>> & w2.<<class1>> and (<<instance_quantifier>>)} = <<world_count>>

scenarios.name: Cardinality Value
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story where, in [no / every / at least / at most / exactly] world(s), [no / every / at least / at most / exactly] instance(s) of [Class1 (source)] is/are connected to [at least / at most / exactly] [n] instances of [Class2 (target)], through association [Association].
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story where, <<world_quantifier>>, <<instance_quantifier>>.

choices.name: every
choices.naturalLanguageSnippet: every instance of <<class1>> is connected to <<count_comparison>> <<n>> instances of <<class2>> through <<association>>

choices.name: no
choices.naturalLanguageSnippet: no instance of <<class1>> is connected to <<count_comparison>> <<n>> instances of <<class2>> through <<association>>

choices.name: at least m
choices.naturalLanguageSnippet: at least <<m>> instances of <<class1>> are connected to <<count_comparison>> <<n>> instances of <<class2>> through <<association>>

choices.name: at most m
choices.naturalLanguageSnippet: at most <<m>> instances of <<class1>> are connected to <<count_comparison>> <<n>> instances of <<class2>> through <<association>>

choices.name: exactly m
choices.naturalLanguageSnippet: exactly <<m>> instances of <<class1>> are connected to <<count_comparison>> <<n>> instances of <<class2>> through <<association>>

scenarios.name: Association Depth
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story where, in [no / every / at least / at most / exactly] world(s), [at least / at most / exactly] [n] instance(s) of [Class] is/are consecutively connected through [Association].
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story where, <<world_quantifier>>, <<chain_comparison>> <<n>> instances of <<class_name>> are consecutively connected through <<association>>.

choices.name: exactly
choices.alloySnippet: (some disj <<x_vars(n)>>: w.<<class_name>> | <<x_chain(n, association)>>) and (no disj <<x_vars(n+1)>>: w.<<class_name>> | <<x_chain(n+1, association)>>)

parameters.name: Number of nodes in the chain

scenarios.name: Universal Class Coverage
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story where every class has at least n instances in every world.
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story where every class has at least <<min_count>> instance(s) in every world.

scenarios.name: Focus Neighbourhood Coverage
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story where, in every world, every class within [k] hops of [Class] (and the class itself) has at least one instance.
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story where, in every world, every class within <<k>> hops of <<class_name>> has at least one instance.

scenarios.name: Universal Association Coverage
scenarios.displayDescription: I want to see a story where every association has at least n pairs in every world.
scenarios.naturalLanguageTemplate: I want to see a story where every association has at least <<min_count>> link(s) in every world.
